role_title: Technical Program Manager – Adversarial Model Research

location.office_time_required: 3 days in the office per week

level_signals: Lead programs across research and engineering teams
Shape programs from the ground up under high ambiguity
Strong experience in technical program management required

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have strong experience in technical program management, with excellent organizational and communication skills.
Are familiar with large language models, prompt engineering, or model evaluation techniques.
Are comfortable managing fast-paced, high-uncertainty projects and shaping them from the ground up.
Are creative and resourceful in devising new methods for testing model behavior and performance.
Can effectively coordinate across technical and non-technical stakeholders to drive alignment and execution.

scope.surface: Adversarial model research and red-teaming evaluations within the Human Data team
primary_metrics_inferred: Identification and mitigation of failure modes
Velocity of red-teaming campaigns and evaluation cycles
core_responsibilities: Lead programs that explore unexpected model behaviors and identify failure modes.
Translate vague or emergent risk signals into clear priorities and actionable research plans.
Design and run creative evaluations, experiments, and red-teaming campaigns.
Collaborate with research, product, and deployment teams to integrate findings into model training and deployment cycles.
Develop repeatable systems for tracking model performance and understanding emerging behavior patterns.

green_flags: Clear product surface and team mission (Human Data team focused on AI safety)
Role sits at the intersection of safety research and program execution — high-impact work
OpenAI is a top-tier AI employer with significant resources and research credibility
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for international applicants
All requirements framed as 'You might thrive if you...' making hard vs. soft classification ambiguous — all treated as hard given unconditional framing in context
No specific years of experience stated — seniority level difficult to calibrate

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from scope of responsibilities and 'strong experience' signal; not stated explicitly)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
ambiguities: All must_haves are drawn from the 'You might thrive in this role if you:' section. This phrasing could imply nice-to-haves, but the items read as baseline competency requirements for the role. Classified as hard severity. Flagged for recruiter confirmation.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; set to 'unclear'.
